summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a high‑energy 1977 show highlighted by a blistering Terrapin>Playing in the Band sequence, Big River and an all‑out first‑set medley.
review: The recording is a clean, crystal‑clear soundboard tape that lets every nuance of the band shine through. Fans rave about the explosive first‑set medley (Eyes → Dancin' → Wharf Rat → Franklin's → Sugar Magnolia) and a scorching Terrapin>Playing in the Band > Samson > Playing in the Band close, plus a standout Big River performance. Overall the show is praised as a “barn‑burner” with flawless playing throughout.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are 4–5 stars and overwhelmingly praise both the musical performance and the recording quality; no negative comments were noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean soundboard tape with no reported drop‑outs or muddiness; reviewers describe it as "absolutely perfect" and flawless.

standout_songs: Terrapin > Playing in the Band > Samson > Playing in the Band
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds with a high‑octane medley that rolls into a powerful second set; reviewers note the energy and experimentation throughout, calling it a “barn burner” with superb pacing.

band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (Godchaux) is highlighted for his entrance around 2:00 and praised as part of the flawless playing.
